mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates signal weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, contradicting the negative directional prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and payrolls indicates robust economic activity that drives corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when both INDPRO and PAYEMS show positive momentum, invalidating the interaction-driven mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal increased construction activity and consumer confidence, which drive demand-side economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative despite HOUST exceeding 1500, refuting the demand-driven hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent disinflation (CPIAUCSL < 260) signals stable price environments that support higher equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when CPIAUCSL remains below 260, invalidating the disinflationary optimism hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS > 540,000) indicates resilient demand that supports corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative despite RSAFS exceeding 540,000, contradicting the demand-side mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 105.7) signals weakening supply-side capacity, which reduces corporate profitability and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive when INDPRO falls below 105.7, invalidating the supply-side pessimism hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in unemployment and increases in payrolls indicate a tightening labor market that drives wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive when both UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rises, refuting the labor market-driven negative hypothesis.
